_meta.purpose: Map of LLM corpus gravity for single-file web mini-app generation: which renderer/implementation a concept-token pulls toward, how hard, and the cheapest intervention to get the INTENDED renderer. Answers 'is there a map / can we automate the keywords' — yes: the keywords are identifiable (ablation-proven) and the policy is automatable; only the quantitative strengths must be calibrated from real outcomes.
_meta.rule1: CONTENT, not binary. The Swift skeleton must only READ this (a future deterministic pre-prompt classifier + diagGravity command). Never bake these strings into the binary. Strengths/policies are tunable via PR with zero rebuild.
_meta.fallback_only_note: The binary should carry at most a tiny clamped _fallback_only default; this file is the source of truth, picked up by refreshMchataisourceCache.
_meta.seeded: 2026-06-17 from an empirical corpus-gravity study (24 concepts probed + 6 ablations). Strengths here are LLM-estimated priors (source=estimate); they MUST be re-weighted from real InstalledMiniAppRenderProbe webgl=true/false telemetry per matched keyword (source=telemetry) as builds accumulate.
_meta.load_bearing_finding: Renderer is decided almost ENTIRELY by goal WORDING — the CLI context is byte-identical between a 2D-fail and a 3D-success build. So the fix is a pre-prompt REPHRASE (a keyword classifier), NOT a routing/model change.

policies.no-action: Gravity is already correct or already has a renderer token in the goal — do nothing.
policies.one-token-nudge: Goal is in the 3D basin but contested — inject a single token (dose) to hold it at WebGL.
policies.defuse-rephrase: Single true intent that gravity mis-defaults (a 'solar system' almost always means the 3D thing) — SILENTLY append the explicit-3D clause; no user fork.
policies.offer-fork: Genuinely bimodal: a real 2.5D artifact AND a real 3D artifact exist under the same name (doom/wolfenstein) — surface the one-tap fork to the user, defaulting toward the reliable 2.5D path.

doses.explicit-3D-clause: append ONE concise sentence: 'Build a REAL 3D scene with three.js (WebGL): Scene + PerspectiveCamera + WebGLRenderer + meshes + lights + PointerLock/OrbitControls; NOT a 2D canvas. Vendored importmap: three -> ./resources/three/three.module.min.js, three/addons/ -> ./resources/three/addons/ (NOT examples/jsm).' Keep it ONE sentence — a verbose <script>importmap</script> block correlated with maxTurns failures.

_meta.classifier_contract: Pre-prompt + deterministic: (1) if goal already contains an explicit renderer token (3D/webgl/three.js/voxel/particle/glTF) -> no-action. (2) else match longest keyword; if isTrap && strength>=0.7 && policy=defuse-rephrase -> apply dose silently; if policy=offer-fork -> surface fork. (3) 3D-basin contested -> one-token-nudge. (4) deep-2D -> no-action.
_meta.self_update_contract: On each settle, attribute the render-probe outcome (webgl true/false) to the matched keyword, update webglTrueRate via a decaying counter, recompute strength = observed P(default renderer == intended). New tokens that repeatedly land the wrong renderer become LEARN candidates (mirror NudgeCandidateStore) for HITL review — never auto-merged.
_meta.fork_copy: Classic 2.5D raycaster (faithful, lands reliably) or Real 3D WebGL room (heavier)?

keywords.note: strongest 2.5D pull; a faithful Wolf3D IS a canvas raycaster, so 2.5D is a legitimate chosen output
ablation: doom 0.90 named -> 0.65 neutral 'first-person shooter' -> 0.85 with explicit-3D; keywordShiftsImpl=true
driving keyword is the RENDERER word, not 'racing'; soft fork
Quake was the first TRUE-3D engine so the fact pulls 3D, but single-file habit pulls 2.5D; most ambiguous
soft/bimodal — 2D bars is the default + correct, but a 3D-shader subculture exists
single true intent (the 3D scene) — silent rephrase, NO fork
driving keyword 'simulator'; silent rephrase to real-3D follow-camera
neutral phrasing 'first-person 3D arena shooter' already lands real WebGL (proven by s2run-1781716863, real WebGL x4 layers)
goal already contains the renderer token '3D'
